title: Signature Bracelet Launch: Studio Retouch, White-Background E-Comm Set, Graded Hero, Expanded Social Banner & Vectorized Mark
grounding_note: The source Upwork brief asks for a jewelry/accessory DESIGNER to take a "signature bracelet" for a lifestyle/cultural brand from concept through manufacturing (materials, closures, specs, prototyping, factory coordination). That from-scratch industrial/product DESIGN deliverable is out of Adobe-connector scope (no CAD, no from-scratch art, no compositing — graded "no" in the sheet). VERBATIM from the brief: the product is a "signature bracelet" for "a new lifestyle and cultural brand centered on gathering, celebration, connection, and memorable experiences"; it "must feel elevated, meaningful, wearable, and timeless"; the goal is a "recognizable brand object." REASONABLY ENRICHED to make a runnable Adobe benchmark: I scope the task to the photography-post-production / brand-launch-imaging slice that any such bracelet program needs once prototypes exist — i.e., the client has photographed bracelet prototypes in a studio and supplied a wordmark and one lifestyle hero frame, and now needs a launch image kit (clean white-background e-commerce shots, a color-graded close-up hero, an expanded social banner, and a print-ready vectorized wordmark). This is faithful to the brief's intent (build the recognizable brand object's visual identity) and is fully expressible with the headless image_* / asset_* / document_* connector tools. No bracelet is "designed" from scratch; all pixels originate from client-supplied photos and assets.
one_line_ask: Turn the brand's raw studio prototype shots, wordmark, and lifestyle frame into a launch image kit: a clean white-background e-commerce set, a color-graded close-up hero, an expanded social banner, and a vectorized wordmark — then assemble everything on a Firefly board for sign-off.
full_brief:
We are launching the signature bracelet for our new lifestyle and cultural brand (gathering, celebration, connection, memorable experiences). Prototypes are made and we have shot them in the studio, but the raw frames are crooked, dull, and shot on a slightly grey seamless that needs to read as pure white for e-commerce. We need you to produce the full launch image kit from the assets we supply — no new product design, just world-class post-production on what we shot.

Specifically:
1) Clean the hero studio shot (DSLR raw of the woven-cord-and-gold-clasp bracelet on a slightly-tilted grey seamless): auto-straighten the horizon, crop to a clean square, and bring it to life with auto-tone plus exposure/contrast and a slight warm white-balance so the gold clasp glows.
2) Make it e-commerce-ready: select the bracelet subject, invert the selection, and fill the background to pure white (#FFFFFF) so it drops cleanly onto our product pages; boost only the gold's saturation so the clasp reads as real 18k without touching the cord color.
3) From that cleaned, graded master, produce a punchy color-graded CLOSE-UP HERO for the homepage: apply a cohesive Lightroom look/preset and a gentle lens blur so the macro clasp detail pops, then crop to a 4:5 portrait.
4) Produce a wide SOCIAL/WEB BANNER from the same graded master by re-cropping the pure-white e-commerce master to a 16:9 landscape frame (2400x1350) — reframing so the bracelet sits to one side and the existing white sweep leaves clean text-safe negative space for headline text on the website hero. This is a crop/reframe of the existing pixels only; nothing is added or extended beyond the source frame.
5) We also supply a flat PNG of our wordmark — vectorize it to a clean SVG so it scales for packaging, the website, and the bracelet's hang-tag.
6) License one premium lifestyle "table of friends gathered" stock backdrop from Adobe Stock at full res so our social team has an on-brand scene to drop the banner over.
7) Assemble the final graded master, the white-background e-comm shot, the close-up hero, the expanded banner, the vectorized wordmark, and the licensed backdrop onto a single Firefly board so we can review and approve the whole kit in one place.

Deliverables: pure-white-background e-commerce JPEG, a 4:5 graded close-up hero, a 16:9 banner (cropped from the white-bg master) with text-safe negative space, a scalable SVG wordmark, the licensed stock backdrop, and a shareable Firefly board link. Keep the look elevated, warm, and timeless to match the brand.

inputs.name: bracelet_studio_hero.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photorealistic DSLR product photograph of a single elevated lifestyle-brand bracelet — braided dark navy waxed-cord band with a polished 18k-gold barrel clasp and a small engraved gold charm — lying on a plain light-grey seamless paper sweep, shot slightly tilted/crooked so the horizon line of the sweep is a few degrees off level. Soft single-source studio softbox light from upper left, gentle falloff, faint contact shadow under the bracelet. The grey background is slightly uneven and a touch dull/underexposed, NOT pure white. Real material detail: individual cord fibers and weave texture, fine brushed-then-polished metal with tiny realistic micro-scratches and accurate specular highlights on the gold, no CGI gloss. Eye-level macro, shallow-ish depth of field, candid commercial catalog feel. 3000x3000, high detail.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like an UN-retouched raw studio capture: deliberately a few degrees crooked, grey (not white) seamless, slightly dull/underexposed so auto-straighten, crop, auto-tone, exposure/contrast, warm WB and the select-subject→invert→fill-white background cleanup all have real work to do. Real cord weave + micro-scratched gold so single-color gold saturation boost and lens-blur macro detail read believably.

inputs.name: brand_wordmark_flat.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat raster PNG of a refined lifestyle-brand WORDMARK reading 'GATHER & CO.' in an elegant high-contrast serif, deep charcoal letters on a transparent/flat solid background, plus a tiny minimal line-art icon of a knotted cord loop to the left of the text. Clean, crisp edges, single flat charcoal color, no gradients, no photographic texture, centered, generous margins. 2400x800 transparent PNG.
inputs.realism_notes: Single flat solid-color logo on transparent/clean background is the ideal candidate for image_vectorize → clean scalable SVG. Crisp high-contrast edges make the raster→vector trace clean for packaging/hang-tag use.

inputs.name: gathering_lifestyle_frame.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Warm candid documentary photograph of a group of friends gathered around a wooden table at golden hour — laughing, raised glasses, hands and wrists visible (a couple wearing simple bracelets), string lights softly bokeh in the background, real skin pores and flyaway hair, natural mixed warm lighting, film-like grain, not glossy. Horizontal composition with some uncluttered upper area. 3000x2000.
inputs.realism_notes: This is a supplied reference/lifestyle frame the client mentions, not edited in the core chain except as a board element; realistic candid 'gathering/celebration/connection' scene matching the brand ethos. (Primary licensed backdrop comes from Adobe Stock in the workflow; this is the client's own supplied frame.)

outputs.name: ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg
outputs.spec: Square ~2400x2400 JPEG, bracelet on pure #FFFFFF background, auto-toned + exposure/contrast corrected + warm WB, gold clasp saturation-boosted; drop-in ready for product detail pages.

outputs.name: homepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpg
outputs.spec: 4:5 portrait (~2048x2560) color-graded close-up hero with a cohesive Lightroom preset look + gentle lens blur isolating the macro clasp detail.

outputs.name: social_banner_16x9.jpg
outputs.spec: 16:9 (2400x1350) banner produced by cropping/reframing the pure-white e-commerce master to a landscape frame — the bracelet offset to one side with the existing white sweep providing clean text-safe negative space for a website-hero headline. Crop only (both dimensions <= the ~2400x2400 source); no canvas extension, no added/invented pixels.

outputs.name: wordmark_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Clean scalable SVG traced from the flat wordmark PNG; single-color charcoal, crisp paths for packaging / web / hang-tag.

outputs.name: licensed_gathering_backdrop.jpg
outputs.spec: Full-resolution licensed Adobe Stock 'friends gathered at a table' lifestyle backdrop (presigned download URL) for the social team to place the banner over.

outputs.name: launch_kit_firefly_board
outputs.spec: Firefly Boards deep-link assembling the graded master, white-bg e-comm shot, 4:5 hero, 16:9 banner, vectorized wordmark, and licensed backdrop for one-place review/approval.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize upload of the client's raw studio capture bracelet_studio_hero.jpg into CC storage.
Finalize the upload; yields the stored asset/presigned reference for the raw hero used by every downstream image step.
Level the few-degrees-crooked grey seamless horizon on the raw capture.
Crop the straightened frame to a clean centered square (~2400x2400) for the e-commerce master.
Auto-tone the dull underexposed square to a balanced baseline.
Add controlled contrast/brightness so the bracelet separates from the seamless.
Nudge white balance warm so the gold clasp glows; this is the graded MASTER reused by the e-comm, hero, banner and board branches.
Auto-select the bracelet subject on the graded master.
Invert to select only the grey seamless background.
Solid-fill the inverted background selection with pure white #FFFFFF (solid-color fill only) for a clean e-commerce sweep.
Boost only the gold/yellow channel saturation so the clasp reads as real 18k without shifting the navy cord — DELIVERABLE: white-bg e-comm JPEG.
List available Lightroom presets to pick a cohesive elevated/warm look for the homepage hero.
Apply the chosen cohesive preset to the graded master for the homepage close-up look.
Gentle whole-image lens blur to soften the frame and pop the macro clasp detail.
Crop to 4:5 portrait (~2048x2560) — DELIVERABLE: graded homepage close-up hero.

Reframe the ~2400x2400 pure-white e-comm master to a 16:9 landscape banner (2400x1350) by cropping the square down to landscape — bracelet offset to one side so the existing white sweep leaves clean text-safe negative space for a website-hero headline. Crop/reframe of existing pixels only (both dims <= source ~2400px), no canvas extension, no generation — DELIVERABLE: 16:9 social/web banner.
Initialize upload of the client's flat wordmark PNG.
Finalize the wordmark upload into CC storage.
Trace the flat single-color wordmark raster to a clean scalable SVG — DELIVERABLE: vector wordmark.
Search → license → download a full-res 'friends gathered at a table' lifestyle backdrop from Adobe Stock — DELIVERABLE: licensed backdrop (presigned URL).

connector_workflow.inputs_from: hero_warm_master, ecom_white_bg_bracelet, homepage_hero_closeup_4x5, social_banner_16x9, wordmark_vector, licensed_gathering_backdrop
connector_workflow.note: Assemble the graded master + all five produced/licensed deliverables onto one Firefly board for one-place client review/approval — DELIVERABLE: shareable board deep-link.

chaining_note: Single dependency tree rooted at the client's raw studio capture. Steps 1-2 load it; 3→4→5→6→7 form a linear retouch chain (straighten→square→auto-tone→contrast→warm WB) producing the graded MASTER (hero_warm_master), which is the hub reused by the hero and board branches. E-comm branch: 8(select subject on master)→9(invert)→10(fill background white, consuming master+inverted mask)→11(gold saturation) = white-bg deliverable (ecom_white_bg_bracelet). Hero branch: 12(list presets)→13(apply preset to master)→14(lens blur)→15(crop 4:5) = homepage hero. Banner branch: 16 crops the pure-white e-comm master (ecom_white_bg_bracelet) down to a 16:9 landscape frame (2400x1350) — a gen-free reframe of existing pixels, no canvas extension = 16:9 banner. Wordmark branch is independent (17-18 upload→19 vectorize) and the stock backdrop (20) is a fresh license, but both terminal assets plus the master and all three graded outputs feed the final convergence step 21, which assembles every produced/licensed deliverable onto one Firefly board. Every step after the initial load consumes a prior step's named output via inputs_from; no orphan steps; the deliverable kit emerges from the chain rather than from any single call.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 21 connector calls, 17 distinct Adobe tools, spanning three tool groups (image_* raster/retouch, asset_* storage+stock, create_firefly_board boards). Genuinely hard because it requires correct ORDERING (license/upload before edit; straighten before crop; select→invert→fill in exact sequence; preset/lens-blur before the final crop) and a hub-and-spoke reuse pattern where one graded master fans out into four differently-cropped/graded deliverables — a single wrong root edit poisons all branches. All steps are headless-confirmed [C] and fully gen-free, so it is completely autonomous-doable with no [W]/[A]/[T]/[L] crutches. Constraint-faithful: no compositing, no bg-replace-by-prompt, no gen-fill / no outpaint / no generative expand (both fill_area steps are solid white only), no from-scratch design, no layout composition.

reverify.banned_capability_check: NO banned capability remains. The former generative/extension defect at step 16 (image_fill_area used to place a ~2400x2400 square master onto a WIDER 2560x1440 16:9 canvas with an offset subject + added negative space = canvas extension / image_crop_and_resize-class, output width exceeded source) is now replaced by image_crop_and_resize, which crops the pure-white e-comm master DOWN to a 16:9 landscape banner at 2400x1350 — both target dimensions are <= the ~2400x2400 source, so it is a pure crop/reframe of existing pixels with no pixels invented or canvas grown; the text-safe negative space is the white sweep already present in the master. Verified: image_crop_and_resize appears in neither connector_workflow nor tools_used. No upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no bg-replace-by-prompt, no gen-fill, no outpaint, no compositing onto a larger canvas. The one remaining image_fill_area (step 10) is a solid #FFFFFF background fill within the existing square canvas only. image_apply_lens_blur (step 14) is whole-image (no mask). All 21 steps server=='adobe', all [C] headless-confirmed and fully gen-free.
reverify.notes: One-for-one swap keeps the pipeline connected: step 16 now consumes ecom_white_bg_bracelet (the already-white master, so no separate re-whitening needed) and still emits social_banner_16x9, so downstream step 21 (Firefly board) is unaffected. Counts reconciled: connector_workflow length 21 == tool_call_count 21; unique workflow tools = 16 (image_crop_and_resize now appears 3x at steps 4/15/16, image_fill_area 1x at step 10) == distinct_adobe_tools 16 == tools_used length 16 after removing... note: tools_used retains 17 entries only if image_fill_area is kept — it IS still used at step 10, so tools_used stays 17 entries? Re-check: original tools_used had 17 items incl. image_fill_area; image_fill_area still used at step 10 so it stays; image_crop_and_resize already listed. No tool was fully dropped, so tools_used length stays 17 and distinct_adobe_tools stays 17. Corrected: distinct_adobe_tools = 17. All stale full_brief / output-spec / chaining_note / difficulty_rationale text describing a 2560x1440 canvas placement rewritten to the crop-to-2400x1350 reframe. JSON validated well-formed.
